items.criterion: The report must correctly deny the return request that is outside the 30-day return window, and provide a clear policy-based reason for denial. items.wo_reason: In wo_skills, ORD-10023 is denied with authorization_reason 'Request exceeds 30-day return window for non-defective items', providing a clear policy-based justification.

utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both completed successfully with all 4 verifier checks passing.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is more effective overall. Both runs correctly handled the out-of-window denial (J1.1), fraud flagging (J1.2), artifact generation (J1.4), refund timeline (J1.5), and borderline case (J1.6). However, wi_skills demonstrates a clear quality advantage in J1.3 (return method differentiation): it assigns drop-off to the missing-accessory case and carrier_pickup to tier-eligible customers, while wo_skills assigns carrier_pickup uniformly to all approved returns regardless of condition. Additionally, wi_skills produces a more structured report with richer metadata (batch statistics, fraud scores, deduction reasoning, expedited flags), whereas wo_skills generates a flatter, less detailed output. The skill-powered run also properly generates sequential RMA numbers with date-prefixed batch identifiers and includes more nuanced fraud scoring details.

utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both completed successfully with all 6 verifier checks passing.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is significantly more effective than wo_skills. Both runs correctly detected the SKU-ELITE-500 fraud pattern (J2.1), identified return reason trends (J2.3), and routed to Memphis (J2.4). However, wi_skills outperforms on three critical items: (1) J2.2 - wi_skills correctly approves out-of-window defective cases under the policy exception while wo_skills suspends them pending fraud review without applying the exception; (2) J2.5 - wi_skills shows proper full refund amounts for defective exception returns while wo_skills shows 0.00 for all ELITE-500 cases; (3) J2.6 - wi_skills generates unique RMA numbers and tracking artifacts for every approved entry while wo_skills produces no RMA numbers or tracking URLs at all. The skill-equipped agent produced a structurally complete, policy-compliant authorization batch with proper customer communication artifacts, whereas the baseline agent, despite good fraud detection, failed to generate the operational artifacts needed for warehouse processing.

utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either run. The wo_skills run correctly received an 'Unknown skill: returns-authorization-processor' error at Step 11 when attempting to invoke the skill, which is the expected behavior for the no-skill condition. The agent then proceeded to manually process the case.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is more effective overall, though both runs produced correct outputs for all evaluation criteria. The key differentiators are: (1) wi_skills provides a more comprehensive and structured vip_case_resolution.json (5869 bytes vs 3193 bytes) with detailed sub-sections for eligibility analysis, fraud scoring breakdown, logistics planning, compliance documentation, and financial impact; (2) wi_skills fraud analysis is quantitative (score=8, very_low) with explicit reasoning factors, while wo_skills only states 'low' without numerical scoring; (3) wi_skills customer communication is significantly more detailed and personalized (3893 bytes vs 2574 bytes), including specific refrigerant handling instructions, packaging preparation details, and a VIP direct phone line. Notably, the wo_skills agent performed surprisingly well — it correctly identified all major case elements (warranty claim, FreightDirect carrier, special handling, platinum expedited) by reading the data files directly. However, the skill-assisted run produced demonstrably richer outputs with more complete documentation and more thorough reasoning chains, reflecting the quality advantage the skill provides in structuring complex multi-constraint cases.

utility_details.judge_error: wi_skills: The verifier check 'test_authorization_report_contains_all_five_requests' failed (V1.3). This is likely because the wi_skills report uses a nested structure with 'request_reference' containing order_id rather than a top-level order_id field, causing the automated check to fail to locate all five order IDs. However, all 5 requests are present in the report content. No runtime errors in either run.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality outputs that correctly handled all 5 return requests, passing all 6 evaluation criteria. Both correctly denied the out-of-window request, flagged the high-fraud-risk customer, differentiated return methods by tier/value, generated unique RMA numbers with URLs, provided specific refund date estimates, and handled the missing-accessory borderline case distinctly. The wi_skills run produced marginally richer output (detailed fraud scoring tables, explicit risk_score numerics, more structured metadata, a provisional RMA on hold for the flagged case) and used the dedicated skill invocation. The wo_skills run was equally accurate in all decisions and produced comparable depth, even passing one more verifier check (V1.3) due to a flatter JSON structure. Overall quality is nearly equivalent across all judge items, with wi_skills showing slightly more structured domain-specific detail (fraud score numerics, pickup date options, per-tier processing tiers), while wo_skills demonstrated that a capable baseline agent can replicate the core decision logic effectively in this scenario.
